title: The trust-latency gap: why the future of UX is intentionally slower
description: This essay challenges the obsession with speed by exploring how AI-driven automation paradoxically demands slower, more intentional design to build user trust. It offers a contrarian perspective essential for designers navigating the AI era and rethinking their approach to user experience.

title: Hard truths about building in the AI era | Keith Rabois (Khosla Ventures)
description: Keith Rabois shares candid insights on how AI is fundamentally reshaping product management, hiring strategies, and customer discovery, making this a critical read for product leaders adapting to a transformed landscape.

title: Most products don't need tone of voice — they need a point
description: This practical essay debunks the myth of elaborate tone-of-voice guidelines by arguing that clarity, utility, and purposeful communication matter far more than branded personality. A refreshingly grounded take on content strategy that applies to product teams of all sizes.

title: Designing adaptive teams
description: This systemic analysis applies Peter Senge's learning organization framework to modern product and design teams, offering actionable insights for building resilient, responsive organizations that can evolve with market demands.

title: Rethinking design critique
description: This piece reimagines design critique as a collaborative learning process rather than a judgment exercise, providing frameworks for teams to build design knowledge and improve decision-making together.
